description: Louis being carried through Africa on a throne in 1960. From a box of 63 prints of Louis Armstrong at his Corona home or abroad between 1930 and 1971.

Louis and Lucille Armstrong walk arm-in-arm down outdoor stairs in Accra, Ghana, Africa. Trummy Young and James Moxon walk a few steps behind them as a crowd looks on. This photo comes from a box containing 67 prints of Louis Armstrong in Africa taken between 1956 and 1961.
Louis, Trummy Young, Jack Lesberg and Edmond Hall playing outdoors for dancers in Ghana in 1956. From a box containing 67 prints of Louis Armstrong in Africa taken between 1956 and 1961.
Nkrumah and Lucille play miniature hockey game while Louis watches. From a box containing 67 prints of Louis Armstrong in Africa taken between 1956 and 1961

Louis wearing African garb with the All Stars in 1960 (Trummy Young, Danny Barcelona, Barney Bigard and Mort Herbert are pictured). The Pepsi-Cola logo is visible behind Louis. From a box of 67 prints of Louis Armstrong in Africa taken between 1956 and 1961.
Louis greets a local dignitary dressed in full African garb and holding a staff. From a box containing 67 prints of Louis Armstrong in Africa taken between 1956 and 1961.

Photo of Louis meeting the Emir and the Madakin of Kano in Kano, Nigeria. They are seated in a large and ornately decorated room in the palace of the Emir of Kano. Includes a letter stating that Louis' tour through Ghana and Nigeria introduced the populace to American jazz and Pepsi. From a box that contains 67 prints of Louis Armstrong in Africa taken between 1956 and 1961.

Photo of Louis Armstrong shaking hands with fans in Accra, Ghana. Includes a letter stating that Louis Armstrong was Pepsi-Cola's Ambassador of Good Will and was being greeted in Accra, Ghana by ""enthusiastic jazz fans."" It also states that he gave concerts to ""record breaking crowds and introduced Pepsi to the populace."" From a box that contains 67 prints of Louis Armstrong in Africa taken between 1956 and 1961.
Photograph of Louis standing in Nigeria in a courtyard and talking to two men in African outfits during a tour in Africa. Louis is holding his suit jacket.

Louis bows toward Edmond Hall and Trummy Young as they play onstage for an outdoor audience on his Africa tour in 1956. An enormous sea of smiling fans is visible behind him. Louis, in shirtsleeves, snaps his fingers and holds a trumpet in one hand. From a box containing 253 snapshots of Louis Armstrong during international tours between 1932 and 1967.
A crane with cameramen extends over a large outdoor audience during filming for Edward R. Murrow's "Satchmo the Great" in West Africa. Audience members, many in hats, stand in rows.

Louis bows to Edmond Hall and Trummy Young and members of the All Stars as they play onstage for an outdoor audience on his Africa tour in 1956. A photographer crouches onstage. Louis, in shirtsleeves, holds his trumpet in one hand.

Louis bows to Edmond Hall and Trummy Young as they play onstage for an outdoor audience on his Africa tour in 1956. An enormous audience of smiling fans is visible behind him. Louis, in shirtsleeves, snaps his fingers and holds a trumpet in one hand. The reverse is stamped with the mark of an Accra photo service.

Edmond Hall, Louis and Trummy Young play onstage for an outdoor audience on his Africa tour in 1956. The photo is taken from behind the musicians, revealing an enormous audience facing the stage. The reverse is stamped with the mark of an Accra photo service.
Lucille dances with a man in traditional dress as Louis and the All Stars play in Accra, Ghana in 1956. Trummy Young is visible, as is Barrett Deems, sitting at a drum set with his name on it.

Louis and members of the All-Stars, including Trummy Young and Edmond Hall perform at the airport in Accra Ghana in 1956. Eugene Bullard, in sunglasses and a black suit, looks on with a large crowd which includes men in airline uniforms, local musicians and photographers. Louis holds his trumpet and smiles broadly.
Louis performs on stage at the Paramount nightclub in Accra, Ghana with Edmond Hall, Trummy Young and others. Louis blows his trumpet into a microphone.

Louis hands his trumpet to a boy sitting at a desk in a classroom in Accra, Ghana in 1956. Other boys in school uniforms crowd around. A boy can be seen in the background, peering through an open window to catch a glimpse. From a box containing 253 snapshots of Louis Armstrong during international tours between 1932 and 1967.

Louis and men in traditional Kenyan dress gather at the Maia Carberry Hospital in Nairobi in the 1950's. Louis, in a suit and bow tie, is seated in a chair. A man in western dress stands behind him, with along with men in white robes and turbans. Reverse side includes note of location. From a box containing 253 snapshots of Louis Armstrong during international tours between 1932 and 1967.

Luis meets a crowd upon his arrival in Accra, Ghana in May of 1956. Louis wears a tuxedo and holds his trumpet. People in military dress are visible behind him. From a box containing 253 snapshots of Louis Armstrong during international tours between 1932 and 1967.

Louis and Lucille and others watch a dancer at a performance in Accra Ghana in 1956. Louis laughs and holds the arm of the dancer as she performs for him. A crowd of people look on, the sky and trees visible above. The reverse includes the note, ""Louis at Atchimota display with the lady reminiscent of May Ann"" and a stamp from the Information Services Department in Accra. From a box containing 253 snapshots of Louis Armstrong during international tours between 1932 and 1967.

Louis blows his trumpet, joined by Edmond Hall, Velma Middleton and local musicians upon his arrival at the airport in Accra, Ghana in 1956. A photographer holds up a camera behind them. The wing of an airplane is visible. From a box containing 253 snapshots of Louis Armstrong during international tours between 1932 and 1967.

Louis blows his trumpet among a dense crowd of club goers at the Paramount Club in Accra, Ghana in May of 1956. All Stars Trummy Young, Edmond Hall and Jack Lesberg are joined by other musicians on the stage behind Louis. Reverse contains info about place and a stamp from the Information Services Department in Accra. From a box containing 253 snapshots of Louis Armstrong during international tours between 1932 and 1967.

Louis, Edmond Hall, Trummy Young, and Barrett Deems perform onstage at an outdoor venue for an enormous crowd in Accra, Ghana in May of 1956. The photo is taken from behind the performers, revealing a crowd that is hundreds deep. Trees can be seen in the background. Reverse contains info about place and a stamp from the Information Services Department in Accra. From a box containing 253 snapshots of Louis Armstrong during international tours between 1932 and 1967.

Louis, in a traditional Nigerian hat, poses for a photo on a street in Nigeria while on tour in 1960. He holds his right fist in the air as he smiles for the camera. He wears a bow tie and shirtsleeves and carries his jacket over his arm. Adults and children, including a man carrying a large bucket on his head, look on as they stand in the street behind him. From a box of 52 oversized images of Louis and/or Lucille Armstrong between 1930 and 1980.

Scrapbook is titled "Satchmo In Caracas Dec. 4, 1957," but actually contains news clippings and magazine articles on Louis and his 1960-1961 trip to Africa and other miscellaneous items. Page contains a clipping of an article about Louis's visit to Congo, where his visit helped halt fighting in the civil war there, and where he was given the honorary name "Okouka," in reference to a wizard who tamed wild beasts with his music.

Scrapbook is titled "Satchmo In Caracas Dec. 4, 1957," but actually contains news clippings and magazine articles on Louis and his 1960-1961 trip to Africa and other miscellaneous items. Page contains a clippings about Louis's 1961 Africa tour. A headline reads, "Satchmo Home, Got Treatment 'Like King.'"

Scrapbook is titled "Satchmo In Caracas Dec. 4, 1957," but actually contains news clippings and magazine articles on Louis and his 1960-1961 trip to Africa and other miscellaneous items. Page contains a news clipping about Louis's 1961 trip to the Congo. It contains a photograph of Louis being carried in a chair by men in traditional dress.

Scrapbook is titled "Satchmo In Caracas Dec. 4, 1957," but actually contains news clippings and magazine articles on Louis and his 1960-1961 trip to Africa and other miscellaneous items. Page contains a clipping, which is largely obscured by tape and a blank piece of paper, of a newspaper article and photo. Part of the name "Langston" (Hughes?) can be seen in a headline, as well as part of the name Satchmo.

Scrapbook belonging to Louis and/or Lucille Armstrong. Compiled by Associated Booking. News clippings: Melody Maker June 9, 1956 article "All-Stars Whoop It Up In Africa" with photo of Louis playing trumpet while walking with Trummy Young and Edmond Hall, also playing their instruments. People following them are all playing trumpet; Photo of Louis, Edmond Hall, and Trummy Young playing before an audience; Same playing with dancers.

Scrapbook belonging to Louis and/or Lucille Armstrong. Page contains a clipping with a photo of Louis and the All Stars at Empress Hall in London. A headline reading "An ambassador called Satchmo" is taped across the photo. Louis, horn in hand, smiles at the audience as he points with one finger.
